title: Mastering Chess and Shogi by Self-Play with a General Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
authors: David Silver, Thomas Hubert, Julian Schrittwieser, Ioannis Antonoglou et al.
hook: AlphaZero mastered chess, shogi, and Go from scratch by playing itself.
promise: You will understand how AlphaZero learned superhuman board play using only self-play and search.
prerequisite_note: Bring an open mind about how computers can learn complex games without expert input.

glossary.plain: A method where an AI learns by playing games against itself, improving over time.
A search algorithm that explores possible moves by simulating random play and building a tree of outcomes.
A neural network that suggests the best moves to play in a given position.
A neural network that predicts the likely outcome of the game from a position.
Learning from scratch without any prior human knowledge or examples.
An approach that works across different games or domains without hand-tuned tricks.

cards.title: AI Masters Games Alone
body: Learn how AlphaZero taught itself chess, shogi, and Go from scratch using only self-play.

cards.title: How can a machine learn to outplay humans?
body: Imagine a computer that starts knowing only the rules of chess, shogi, or Go, and nothing else.
Instead of learning from human games, it plays against itself, gradually discovering strategies that even grandmasters respect.

cards.title: From blank slate to board mastery
body: AlphaZero begins with random moves and no knowledge, then improves by repeatedly playing itself.
It uses deep neural networks and search to evaluate positions, learning which moves lead to victory.
cards.caption: AlphaZero learns entirely from its own experience, not from human examples.

cards.title: Human-crafted vs. self-taught
body: Toggle to see the difference between classic chess engines and AlphaZero.
cards.offNote: Relies on human-designed rules and evaluation functions, requiring decades of expert tuning.
cards.onNote: Learns strategies and evaluations entirely by playing itself, with no human input beyond the rules.

cards.title: Learning by playing yourself
body: AlphaZero's central trick is to blend deep learning with search and self-play, creating a feedback loop of improvement.
Each iteration, it updates its neural networks based on self-play outcomes, steadily refining its sense for good moves and positions.

cards.title: How much search is enough?
body: Adjust the slider to see how search depth affects learning and performance.
cards.low: Learns quickly but overlooks subtle strategies and deeper tactics.
cards.mid: Balances speed and depth, finding strong but not perfect moves.
cards.high: Finds nuanced strategies and rare tactics but requires much more computation.

cards.title: What does AlphaZero need?
body: Select the essential ingredients that let AlphaZero master board games.

choices.label: Self-play
choices.note: Enables learning from its own experiences.

choices.label: Policy Network
choices.note: Suggests which moves to consider in each position.

choices.label: Value Network
choices.note: Estimates the likelihood of winning from any board state.

choices.label: Tabula Rasa Start
choices.note: Begins with zero knowledge except for the rules.

choices.label: General Algorithm
choices.note: Applies the same method to chess, shogi, and Go.

cards.title: AlphaZero's astonishing results
body: Within 24 hours, AlphaZero surpassed champion programs in chess, shogi, and Go, all using the same approach.
Its play style was creative, aggressive, and sometimes surprising even to top human players.

bars.label: Training time (hours)
bars.note: Superhuman in less than one day

cards.caption: AlphaZero achieved dominance in three games with a single, unified algorithm.

cards.title: Why AlphaZero changed AI history
body: AlphaZero showed that an AI could reach superhuman skill in complex domains without any human playbooks.
Its general approach inspires new ways to tackle problems beyond board games, using learning and search together.

recap.label: Start
recap.note: Knows only the rules, no human strategies.

recap.label: Search
recap.note: Combines neural networks with tree search.

cards.title: Mastering Chess and Shogi by Self-Play with a General Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
body: Read the original AlphaZero paper for all the technical details and experimental results.
